hypothesis: Large document-shaped workbooks need ZIP/XML manifesting and read-only targeted sampling before expensive workbook loading.
actions: Generated workbook manifest from OOXML package metadata.
Generated read-only sampled row windows from every sheet, including manifest-derived structural windows.
effectiveness_signals: Manifest identified pivot tables, images, external links, and the oversized 누적 sheet before full extraction.
Read-only sampling provided enough values and formula text for initial deterministic seeds.
process_decision: Keep as the required first stage.
process_learning: The manifest must remain the workbook package authority for pivot, drawing, external link, and large-sheet risk discovery.
next_adjustment: Use manifest and sample outputs as inputs to all downstream evidence profiles.

hypothesis: Formula signature profiling can group raw formula relations into reviewable dependency patterns, especially for large sheets and repeated formulas.
actions: Generated formula pattern profile using relative-reference signatures.
Grouped formula relations by source block, reference kind, target sheet/workbook, function, and formula signature.
effectiveness_signals: 누적 sheet SUBTOTAL formulas were grouped into a summary_formula_band pattern.
Raw formula relation noise became more reviewable through relation_groups.
process_decision: Keep formula pattern profiling as a pre-split and relation-review stage.
process_learning: Raw relation evidence should remain available, while grouped relations become the human-review projection.
next_adjustment: Use formula signatures as split/merge evidence for 2D cell regions.

hypothesis: Row-oriented seeds are insufficient because side-by-side tables can share rows and vertically adjacent tables can touch without blank separators.
actions: Added cell_regions as 2D projections over row-oriented seeds.
Added blank-column and touching repeated-header split candidates.
Updated HTML viewer to expose 2D cell region candidates and split evidence.
effectiveness_signals: 수익인식60일, 수익인식60일_강사료, and 정가표 produced blank-column split candidates.
The sample workbook did not trigger touching-header candidates, but fixture tests confirmed the gate behavior.
process_decision: Keep row_band only as an early seed; promote 2D cell_region as the review concept.
process_learning: Physical adjacency is not enough to merge regions, and physical separation is not enough to split them permanently.
next_adjustment: Add style, merged-cell, and render-capture evidence before accepting split decisions.

hypothesis: Semantic ontology generation requires domain knowledge, but reusable general-domain knowledge and boundary-scoped local-domain knowledge must be separated.
actions: Updated design to define general domain and local domain layers.
Replaced company-specific wording with declared boundary scoping.
Added domain-layer evidence requirements for semantic ontology candidates.
effectiveness_signals: The design now prevents local terms from being silently promoted beyond their applicability boundary.
Semantic claims must record domain evidence and applicability boundary.
process_decision: Keep domain-layer separation as a semantic ontology gate input.
process_learning: Domain knowledge is evidence, not hidden LLM intuition.
next_adjustment: Define concrete domain knowledge source schemas after structural parsing stabilizes.

hypothesis: Merged ranges, row/column dimensions, and visual style discontinuities can provide deterministic evidence for 2D region boundary gates without immediately forcing splits.
actions: Added structural style profile extraction from worksheet XML and styles.xml.
Generated structural style profile for the sample workbook.
Linked style_discontinuity_boundary evidence into cell_region_split_candidates.
Updated HTML viewer with a Structural Style Profile section.
effectiveness_signals: Style profile surfaced many repeated visual boundaries, especially in calculation-heavy sheets.
누적 remained skipped because the worksheet XML is too large for this pass.
Style evidence is useful but noisy enough that it should remain candidate evidence, not an automatic split rule.
process_decision: Keep structural style profiling as evidence, but add a later ranking/filtering gate before accepting style-based splits.
process_learning: Visual style discontinuity is a high-recall signal; it needs merge/split gate calibration against render capture and formula locality.
next_adjustment: Add merged-cell/title boundary linking and then render-capture cross-validation for high-priority candidate regions.

hypothesis: Split candidates need a ranking/filtering gate because blank columns, repeated headers, style discontinuities, and merged ranges have different evidence strength.
actions: Added boundary_gate_results to workbook block candidates.
Scored split candidates into strong_candidate, review_candidate, and weak_signal.
Linked merged ranges as title/header boundary evidence rather than automatic split decisions.
Updated HTML viewer to show boundary gate results per sheet.
effectiveness_signals: Blank-column splits were promoted to strong candidates.
Style-only discontinuities were mostly retained as review candidates or weak signals instead of automatic splits.
Merged ranges produced title/header evidence without forcing a table split.
process_decision: Keep boundary gate ranking as the filter between high-recall parser evidence and final document graph claims.
process_learning: The process needs separate candidate evidence and gate status layers; otherwise high-recall style signals would over-split tables.
next_adjustment: Use render capture and formula locality to confirm or demote review_candidate boundaries.

hypothesis: Formula/dataflow extraction must produce table-level input/output pipelines, not only cell-level references or relation groups.
actions: Clarified the high-level pipeline by adding Table I/O Pipeline Extraction.
Documented table-level pipeline roles such as input, parameter, bridge, transform, summary, report, and audit_check.
Added a Table I/O Pipeline Gate to deterministic gate design.
Updated AGENTS.md to preserve this requirement in future work.
effectiveness_signals: The existing relation_groups are useful evidence but not yet enough to explain workbook data movement at table/pipeline level.
The process now explicitly separates visual block understanding from formula-driven input/output pipeline understanding.
process_decision: Keep formula/dataflow graph extraction and add table I/O pipeline extraction as a required downstream projection.
process_learning: Visual hierarchy and calculation hierarchy can diverge; both must be represented before semantic ontology generation.
next_adjustment: Implement table_io_pipelines from relation_groups, pivot cache sources, external references, and validated 2D cell regions.

hypothesis: Formula relation groups and pivot cache source relations can be projected into output-centered table I/O pipeline candidates that are easier to review than raw cell references.
actions: Added workbook_table_io_pipeline.py to project relation_groups and pivot cache source relations into table_io_pipeline candidates.
Added workbook-table-io-pipeline.schema.json and fixture tests.
Updated the HTML viewer with a Table I/O Pipelines section.
Regenerated block candidates after tightening formula reference parsing for arithmetic before sheet references.
Generated the sample table I/O pipeline artifact.
effectiveness_signals: Pivot tables are now represented as report pipelines backed by pivot cache source ranges instead of plain value tables.
Formula-backed outputs are grouped into table-level candidates with input refs, transform refs, confidence, and review flags.
The tokenizer fix removed fake target sheets such as B26-누적, C26-결제상세, and D26-결제상세 from relation groups.
process_decision: Keep table_io_pipelines as a reviewable candidate projection, but treat role labels and unresolved inputs as gate inputs rather than accepted final graph claims.
process_learning: Pipeline extraction is useful immediately, but its quality depends on formula tokenizer correctness and 2D region coverage. Parser errors should surface as gate/review signals, not be hidden by the pipeline layer.
next_adjustment: Use the remaining unresolved input-region pipeline and high-priority pivot/report pipelines to decide the first render-capture and visual/formula cross-validation targets.

hypothesis: Before expensive Excel render capture, the parser should produce a deterministic target plan that prioritizes unresolved dataflow, pivot reports, boundary review candidates, repeated formulas, and image hierarchy candidates.
actions: Added workbook_cross_validation_plan.py to generate capture targets and pending visual/formula gate checks.
Added workbook-cross-validation-plan.schema.json and fixture tests.
Regenerated the sample cross-validation plan artifact.
Updated the HTML viewer with a Cross-Validation Plan section and a sheet-diverse recommended first capture batch.
Updated the living design document and implementation map.
effectiveness_signals: All 14 sheets now appear in the capture target universe rather than only the visually dense sheets.
The first batch avoids being dominated by one pivot-heavy sheet by selecting sheet-diverse high/medium targets first.
Unresolved formula input, pivot cache reports, boundary candidates, and image/table hierarchy are now explicit pending gate categories.
process_decision: Keep cross-validation planning as the bridge between deterministic structural artifacts and actual render capture.
process_learning: Capture should be treated as an evidence acquisition step with a work order. Otherwise the process risks spending capture effort on visually obvious regions while missing formula/dataflow uncertainties.
next_adjustment: Run the first capture batch through an Excel render path, normalize capture bboxes back to cell ranges, and record pass/fail gate results.

hypothesis: The recommended first capture batch can be rendered from a sandbox copy of the workbook through Microsoft Excel and attached as visual evidence to pending gate checks.
actions: Added excel_copy_ranges_png.applescript to capture target ranges using Excel copy-picture PNG output.
Added workbook_render_capture.py to open a sandbox workbook copy, run the first capture batch, and assemble render capture artifacts.
Added workbook-render-captures.schema.json and fixture tests.
Captured the 12 recommended first-batch targets from the sample workbook.
Updated the HTML viewer with a Render Captures section.
effectiveness_signals: Excel copy-picture capture succeeded for all first-batch targets without modifying the source workbook.
The HTML viewer now shows actual visual evidence next to workbook structure, pipeline, and gate planning artifacts.
Wide or sparse ranges surfaced capture-quality issues, including very short captures such as 포도 A1:R22 at 1082x19 pixels.
process_decision: Keep Excel copy-picture PNG capture as the first working render path, but do not treat capture existence as semantic gate acceptance.
process_learning: Range-level image capture is enough to start human review, but capture sufficiency needs its own quality gate. Some ranges need tiling, better viewport expansion, or alternate capture parameters before bbox normalization can be trusted.
next_adjustment: Add capture quality checks, then run bbox/visual-feature normalization for the captured first batch and convert pending gate checks into accepted/rejected/review-required outcomes.

hypothesis: Human review of table I/O candidates improves when the same pipeline artifact is visible both as a table and as graph-shaped input/output flow evidence.
actions: Added Mermaid rendering support to the workbook understanding HTML viewer.
Added a sheet-level aggregate graph covering all table I/O pipelines.
Added a pipeline-level detail graph for prioritized output refs and their input refs.
Regenerated the sample HTML review viewer.
effectiveness_signals: The viewer now exposes 결제상세 as the major upstream source feeding pivot-cache report outputs in 결제&수수료 and 매출.
Formula-backed summary/self-sheet flows and pivot-cache report flows are visually distinguishable by edge labels.
The Mermaid source is retained in the HTML for review even if the browser cannot load the renderer.
process_decision: Keep graph visualization as a review surface over the existing table_io_pipelines artifact, not as a separate authority.
process_learning: Sheet-level aggregation helps spot workbook-scale data movement quickly, while output-ref detail remains necessary for reviewing pivot/table candidates.
next_adjustment: Use the graph to choose which high-value pipeline paths should receive capture-quality checks and gate execution next.

hypothesis: The evolving parser process needs an explicit tasklist so implementation order, current status, and next work remain aligned with the design and process ledger.
actions: Added a document-shaped Excel understanding tasklist with stage statuses and current sample artifacts.
Linked the tasklist from the design document and AGENTS.md.
Updated the implementation map to show the tasklist as an active tracking artifact.
effectiveness_signals: The current process now has a single operational view separate from the design baseline.
Future stage additions, deletions, reorderings, splits, merges, or scope changes have an explicit update target.
process_decision: Keep the tasklist as the active execution view and keep the design document as the conceptual baseline.
process_learning: A process ledger explains why the process changed, while a tasklist keeps the current execution sequence easy to review before each next step.
next_adjustment: Before implementing capture quality checks, use the tasklist as the reference point and update it if the next stage is split or re-scoped.

hypothesis: Range PNG capture existence is not enough for downstream visual gates; captures must be scored for readability, shape, and tiling or expanded-window needs before bbox normalization trusts them.
actions: Added workbook_capture_quality.py to evaluate capture PNG dimensions, row/column pixel density, aspect ratio, visible pixel ratio, and recapture recommendations.
Added workbook-capture-quality.schema.json and fixture tests.
Generated the sample capture quality artifact from the first-batch render captures.
Updated the HTML viewer with a Capture Quality section.
Updated the tasklist, design document, AGENTS.md, and implementation map.
effectiveness_signals: The gate surfaced 당월산식 A1:BA22 and 포도 A1:R22 as recapture-required because rendered row height and aspect ratio are too poor for reliable visual gates.
Wide but readable captures such as 결제상세 A1:AY22 and 유하다요_강사료 A1:AI22 are separated into review-required rather than hard failure.
Eight captures can proceed to the next visual gate without immediate recapture.
process_decision: Keep capture quality as a deterministic gate between render capture and coordinate normalization.
process_learning: Capture existence and capture sufficiency are different evidence states. Very wide ranges may need tiling even when the PNG exists and contains visible content.
next_adjustment: Implement capture tiling or viewport expansion for recapture-required and review-required ranges, starting with 당월산식 and 포도.

hypothesis: Recapture repair should be treated as candidate generation plus empirical capture-quality comparison, not as a deterministic final plan.
actions: Revised capture quality recommendations so column tiling is recommended only for wide-range issues, while thin-row captures get visible-row or expanded-window candidates.
Added workbook_recapture_candidate_plan.py to generate alternative recapture candidates from quality results.
Added workbook-recapture-candidate-plan.schema.json and fixture tests.
Generated 12 recapture candidates and captured all candidates through Microsoft Excel.
Generated recapture candidate quality results and added Recapture Candidates / Results sections to the HTML viewer.
Updated tasklist, design, AGENTS.md, implementation map, and process ledger.
effectiveness_signals: Column tiles for 결제상세 and 유하다요_강사료 all became usable captures, confirming tiling is useful for wide but otherwise visible ranges.
Same-window control confirmed 당월산식 and 포도 failures are stable, not transient capture failures.
Expanded-row and visible-row candidates for 당월산식 and 포도 still failed because hidden/collapsed rows remain a view-state issue rather than a simple range-size issue.
process_decision: Keep recapture candidate experiments as the way to test repair strategies, and split hidden row / view-state handling into the next stage before coordinate normalization.
process_learning: Deterministic candidate generation is useful, but deterministic final range selection is not reliable without empirical capture and review. Hidden rows require an authority decision: visible-render truth, structural data truth, or non-authoritative unhide diagnostics.
next_adjustment: Add hidden row / view-state handling that records hidden-row evidence and decides whether to exclude hidden rows from visual gates, use structural data evidence, or create separately labeled unhide diagnostic captures.

hypothesis: Thin or blank captures for filtered sheets can be explained by workbook view-state evidence and should not be repaired by blindly selecting larger ranges.
actions: Added workbook_view_state_profile.py to extract hidden rows, hidden columns, outline columns, autoFilter, filterMode, panes, and capture-window overlap evidence from workbook XML.
Added workbook-view-state-profile.schema.json and fixture tests.
Generated the sample view-state profile from first-batch and recapture capture quality artifacts.
Updated the HTML viewer with a Hidden Row / View-State section.
Updated the tasklist, design document, AGENTS.md, implementation map, and process ledger.
effectiveness_signals: 당월산식 A1:BA22 has 18 hidden rows in the 22-row capture window and 18 hidden columns, explaining the too-thin capture under current visible state.
당월산식 visible-row recapture tiles A21:R100, S21:AJ100, and AK21:BA100 are fully hidden row windows, so larger range selection cannot fix the visual evidence.
포도 capture windows are dominated by filtered or hidden rows, confirming that recapture failure reflects current workbook view-state rather than transient Excel copy-picture failure.
수익인식60일 has a usable capture that is still affected by one filtered row, proving view-state handling must annotate usable windows too.
process_decision: Keep visible render authority and structural data authority separate. Current visible-state captures remain authoritative for what the user sees, while hidden rows remain available for structural extraction and dataflow evidence. Unhide or clear-filter captures may be created only as explicitly non-authoritative diagnostics.
process_learning: View-state profiling prevents the process from treating hidden filtered rows as missing data or as capture-tool failure. Coordinate normalization must consume view-state annotations so hidden rows are not accidentally converted into visual absence claims.
next_adjustment: Proceed to coordinate normalization for usable visible captures and add a diagnostic-capture branch only if hidden-row regions need visual inspection beyond structural data evidence.

hypothesis: Hidden/filter/outline view-state is an input-model preflight concern, not only a capture failure afterthought.
actions: Changed the tasklist so Workbook View-State Preflight runs immediately after Fast ZIP/XML Manifest.
Kept View-State / Capture Reconciliation later in the pipeline after recapture candidate experiments.
Changed workbook_view_state_profile.py so it can run with no capture quality artifact and produce a preflight-only inventory.
Generated mbp-2026-02-view-state-preflight.json for the sample workbook.
Updated design, AGENTS.md, implementation map, and the HTML viewer to record the new ordering and visible-state preservation principle.
effectiveness_signals: The parser can now know hidden/filter state before row sampling, capture planning, recapture candidate generation, and visual interpretation.
The process no longer implies that hidden rows should be revealed before parsing.
The source visible workbook state remains the default human-perception authority.
process_decision: Preserve the original visible state. Do not reveal all hidden rows or clear filters as preprocessing. Inventory hidden/filter/outline state early, and create reveal or clear-filter views only as explicitly non-authoritative diagnostics when structural evidence is insufficient.
process_learning: Revealing everything would improve structural visibility but destroy the author's presentation state and human-perceived document units. Early inventory gives the parser both views of the problem without mutating source authority.
next_adjustment: Run coordinate normalization using view-state annotations so hidden rows are not converted into visual absence claims.

hypothesis: Capture bboxes can be mapped back to workbook cell ranges only after capture quality and view-state evidence distinguish usable visible ranges from hidden/filter-dominated windows.
actions: Added workbook_coordinate_normalization.py to combine render captures, capture quality, and view-state reconciliation.
Added workbook-coordinate-normalization.schema.json and fixture tests.
Generated mbp-2026-02-coordinate-normalization.json from first-batch and recapture candidate captures.
Updated the HTML viewer with a Coordinate Normalization section.
Updated the tasklist, design document, AGENTS.md, implementation map, and process ledger.
effectiveness_signals: Usable and tiled visible captures now have range-level bbox evidence for downstream visual feature detection.
Hidden/filter-dominated captures for 당월산식 and 포도 are blocked from being treated as visual absence.
A usable but filtered capture is retained with a view-state warning instead of silently passing as fully visible.
process_decision: Coordinate normalization is range-level evidence only. It does not detect internal visual features or accept semantic claims. Hidden/filter-dominated captures remain blocked until structural evidence or explicitly non-authoritative diagnostic captures are used.
process_learning: Coordinate mapping must consume view-state annotations. Otherwise, a valid hidden row in workbook structure could be misread as absent content in the visual layer.
next_adjustment: Proceed to visual feature detection over normalized visible ranges, preserving review-required and view-state-warning mappings for human review.

hypothesis: Deterministic image features can prepare visual evidence for later gates without assigning document semantics.
actions: Added workbook_visual_feature_detection.py to extract image metrics, content bboxes, line candidates, dominant colors, and layout signals from normalized visible capture PNGs.
Added workbook-visual-feature-detection.schema.json and fixture tests.
Generated mbp-2026-02-visual-features.json from coordinate normalization and render capture artifacts.
Updated the HTML viewer with a Visual Feature Detection section.
Updated the tasklist, design document, AGENTS.md, implementation map, and process ledger.
effectiveness_signals: All normalized visible captures produced content bboxes and grid/table-like line signals.
The usable but filtered 수익인식60일 capture produced features with an explicit view-state warning.
The 9 hidden/filter-dominated captures remained skipped, preserving the rule that hidden rows are not visual absence.
process_decision: Keep visual feature detection as deterministic image evidence for later gates. It may identify content bboxes and visual line/grid signals, but it must not assign block hierarchy or semantic roles by itself.
process_learning: Normalized visible captures are sufficient for first-pass visual feature extraction. Quality-review and view-state-blocked captures need separate review or diagnostic paths before their features can be trusted.
next_adjustment: Run visual/data/formula cross-validation gates that combine visual features, workbook structure, formulas, pivot dependencies, and view-state decisions into accepted/rejected/review-required outcomes.

hypothesis: Current visual feature evidence can execute a subset of planned gates while safely retaining uncaptured, view-state-blocked, and quality-risk gates as review-required.
actions: Added workbook_cross_validation_gate_execution.py to combine cross-validation plan gates with visual feature evidence.
Added workbook-cross-validation-gate-execution.schema.json and fixture tests.
Generated mbp-2026-02-gate-execution.json for the current sample evidence set.
Updated the HTML viewer with a Cross-Validation Gate Execution section.
Updated the tasklist, design document, AGENTS.md, implementation map, and process ledger.
effectiveness_signals: 17 gates with deterministic visual evidence were accepted, primarily boundary, formula summary, and formula region coherence gates.
49 gates remain review-required because their targets have not yet been captured, including many pivot and image hierarchy candidates.
4 gates remain review-required because hidden/filter view-state blocks trustworthy visual absence claims.
No gates were rejected in this pass because the current evidence did not contain deterministic contradiction signals.
process_decision: Keep gate execution conservative: accepted means the evidence gate passed, not that the document graph is finalized. Missing capture evidence and unsupported object hierarchy gates remain review-required instead of being guessed.
process_learning: The first evidence batch is enough to advance some formula and boundary gates, but pivot visual alignment and image hierarchy need broader capture coverage or specialized visual/object features before deterministic acceptance.
next_adjustment: Proceed to boundary acceptance/rejection using accepted gate evidence while keeping review-required gates attached as unresolved review items.

hypothesis: Semantic ontology construction for the current accounting sample needs an explicit reusable general-domain evidence source, but that source must not override workbook evidence or local boundary evidence.
actions: Inspected /Users/kangmin/.onto/domains/accounting-kr and confirmed 8 domain documents are available.
Recorded the sample-specific general-domain source in the design document.
Updated the active tasklist and implementation map so the domain-source binding is visible before semantic ontology stages begin.
Updated AGENTS.md with the current sample boundary rule.

result_summary.scope: sample-specific general-domain evidence for Korean accounting semantic ontology alignment

effectiveness_signals: The semantic ontology stage now has an explicit general-domain source instead of relying on hidden model intuition.
The design still requires every accepted semantic candidate to point back to workbook evidence.
The rule keeps reusable accounting concepts separate from organization, project, team, tenant, or workbook-family local terms.
process_decision: For this sample workbook only, allow /Users/kangmin/.onto/domains/accounting-kr as general-domain evidence during semantic ontology construction. Do not treat it as source workbook truth or local-domain authority.
process_learning: Domain knowledge should enter the pipeline as an evidence source with a declared scope and applicability boundary. This makes later deterministic gates and human review able to distinguish workbook facts, reusable accounting constraints, and local vocabulary.
next_adjustment: When semantic ontology generation begins, attach explicit general-domain refs to aligned concepts and keep unresolved or workbook-specific terms in the local-domain candidate layer.

hypothesis: Boundary candidates should be promoted into graph-boundary decisions only when deterministic structural evidence is strong enough and current gate evidence does not contradict it.
actions: Added workbook_boundary_decisions.py to combine block boundary candidates with cross-validation gate execution results.
Added workbook-boundary-decisions.schema.json and fixture tests.
Generated mbp-2026-02-boundary-decisions.json for the current sample evidence set.
Updated the HTML viewer with a Boundary Acceptance / Rejection section.
Updated the tasklist, design document, AGENTS.md, implementation map, and process ledger.
effectiveness_signals: All 6 strong blank-column materialized split boundaries were accepted as graph-boundary candidates.
No style-only discontinuity was over-promoted into an accepted graph boundary.
111 candidates remain review-required, making unresolved evidence explicit for human tuning instead of hiding it in parser heuristics.
Region-level accepted gate evidence is no longer treated as proof of every candidate boundary inside that region.
process_decision: Use boundary acceptance as a conservative graph-boundary candidate stage, not as final document graph assembly. Strong blank-column splits can be accepted when no deterministic gate rejects them; style-only, merged-title, missing-capture, and view-state-risk boundaries remain review items.
process_learning: The previous gate execution artifact was too coarse to finalize many fine-grained style boundaries. Boundary decisions need candidate-specific evidence and should preserve unresolved review pressure for capture planning and human feedback.
next_adjustment: Proceed to pipeline role validation using accepted split boundaries as usable structural constraints while retaining review-required boundaries as uncertainty annotations.

hypothesis: Pipeline roles can be validated deterministically from formula signatures, pivot cache authority, input/output references, accepted boundaries, and gate contradictions without requiring every visual capture to be complete.
actions: Added workbook_pipeline_role_validation.py to validate table I/O pipeline roles from pipeline, gate, and boundary artifacts.
Added workbook-pipeline-role-validation.schema.json and fixture tests.
Generated mbp-2026-02-pipeline-role-validation.json for the current sample evidence set.
Updated the HTML viewer with a Pipeline Role Validation section.
Updated the tasklist, design document, AGENTS.md, implementation map, and process ledger.
effectiveness_signals: All 24 pivot-cache-backed report roles were accepted from pivot cache authority rather than treating pivot displays as raw data.
10 summary roles were accepted from SUBTOTAL or SUMIFS formula evidence.
10 transform roles were accepted from formula-signature evidence.
The one unresolved input-region pipeline remained review-required instead of being forced into an accepted role.
34 role validations retain linked gate review annotations without letting missing capture erase formula or pivot authority.
process_decision: Validate pipeline role labels separately from final graph assembly. Missing visual capture remains a review annotation when formula or pivot authority supports the role, but unresolved input ownership blocks acceptance.
process_learning: Role validation should be evidence-specific: pivot report roles depend on cache/source definitions, summary roles depend on formula signatures, and transform roles depend on formula groups. Visual gates are useful for alignment but are not always the primary role authority.
next_adjustment: Proceed to workbook evidence package assembly, bundling workbook facts, visual evidence, gate results, boundary decisions, pipeline role decisions, and lineage refs as the parser input authority.

hypothesis: Later document ontology and LLM proposal stages need one traceable parser input authority that bundles prior deterministic artifacts without reopening or mutating the source workbook.
actions: Extended workbook_evidence.py with artifact-first evidence package assembly.
Updated workbook-evidence.schema.json to validate artifact inventories, decision indexes, review queue, domain refs, lineage refs, and normalized capture mappings.
Generated mbp-2026-02-evidence-package.json for the current sample evidence set.
Updated the HTML viewer with a Workbook Evidence Package section.
Updated the tasklist, design document, AGENTS.md, implementation map, and process ledger.
effectiveness_signals: The package exposes one authority surface for subsequent document ontology mapping.
All prior deterministic artifacts are inventoried with summaries and lineage refs.
Review-required gates, boundaries, and pipeline roles are preserved as a review queue instead of being hidden or guessed away.
The accounting-kr general-domain documents are attached as separate domain refs, preserving separation from workbook and local-domain evidence.
process_decision: Use artifact-first evidence assembly for this sample. Do not reopen the large source workbook for Stage 20; assemble from manifest, read-only samples, visual evidence, gate outputs, boundary decisions, role validations, and domain refs.
process_learning: The evidence package is the right handoff point between deterministic parsing and ontology mapping. It makes unresolved evidence visible and keeps workbook facts, visual captures, formula authority, role decisions, and domain knowledge in separate but linked layers.
next_adjustment: Proceed to document ontology mapping using the evidence package as the sole parser input authority and preserving every accepted node/relation/data view evidence ref.

hypothesis: The document-structure ontology should be applied deterministically to the evidence package before any LLM or semantic ontology generation work begins.
actions: Added workbook_document_ontology_mapping.py to map evidence package artifacts into document-structure ontology nodes, relations, data views, visual evidence, and review items.
Added workbook-document-ontology-mapping.schema.json and fixture tests.
Generated mbp-2026-02-document-ontology-mapping.json for the current sample evidence set.
Updated the HTML viewer with a Document Ontology Mapping section and Mermaid data-view graph.
Updated the tasklist, design document, AGENTS.md, implementation map, and process ledger.
effectiveness_signals: The mapping makes structural ontology claims reviewable before semantic ontology generation.
Pivot tables remain pivot_report_view data views backed by pivot cache/source evidence rather than raw data tables.
The one unresolved-input pipeline remains review_required instead of being forced into an accepted data view.
Every accepted node, relation, and data view is required to retain evidence refs.
process_decision: Keep document ontology mapping as deterministic ontology utilization. Do not generate semantic concepts or use domain knowledge in this stage; preserve review-required evidence for later human or LLM-assisted stages.
process_learning: A separate document ontology projection clarifies which claims are structural and evidence-backed before semantic interpretation begins. It also exposes the large range-ref surface created by formula relations, which may need compaction or review projection tuning later.
next_adjustment: Proceed to the domain knowledge source model, representing general-domain and boundary-scoped local-domain sources before LLM proposal generation.

hypothesis: A document ontology becomes genuinely actionable only when statuses and review reasons are converted into explicit next actions with owners, evidence requirements, gates, and completion effects.
actions: Added workbook_action_contracts.py to project document ontology mapping statuses into action contracts.
Added workbook-action-contracts.schema.json and fixture tests.
Generated mbp-2026-02-action-contracts.json for the current sample evidence set.
Updated the HTML viewer with an Action Contract Layer section and Mermaid action flow graph.
Inserted Action Contract Layer into the active tasklist before Domain Knowledge Source Model and updated design, AGENTS.md, implementation map, and process ledger.
effectiveness_signals: Accepted data views now have ready contracts for downstream graph assembly.

Review-required items now map to concrete actions such as acquire_render_capture, recapture_or_tile_range, reconcile_view_state_authority, resolve_input_region_ownership, and corroborate_boundary_evidence.
The remaining unresolved input path is now high-priority deterministic parser work instead of a passive review label.
View-state-blocked items are explicitly blocked from visual absence claims until authority is reconciled.
process_decision: Keep action contracts as an actionable projection layer. Contracts direct the next step but do not accept new structural or semantic claims. Domain refs remain unused here and are reserved for later semantic stages.
process_learning: Actionability should be a first-class artifact, not only prose in the HTML. This makes process tuning easier because open, blocked, and ready work can be counted and assigned before LLM proposal generation.
next_adjustment: Proceed to Domain Knowledge Source Model with action contracts available as the operational queue for unresolved structural work.

hypothesis: Semantic ontology proposal generation needs explicit domain-source boundaries so reusable accounting knowledge, workbook evidence, and local vocabulary are not conflated.

process_decision: Keep LLM proposal generation as an interpretation layer only. Do not accept semantic concepts, hierarchy edges, aliases, or relations here; Stage 25 must run deterministic source-trace, ontology, coordinate, formula, pivot, local-domain, and conflict gates.
process_learning: LLM interpretation becomes safer when it operates after evidence packaging and domain-source modeling. The high alias count is useful for review, but Stage 25 should aggressively validate aliases and quarantine local-boundary claims until explicit local sources are available.
next_adjustment: Proceed to deterministic validation of LLM proposals and produce accepted, rejected, warning, quarantined, or requires_human_review outcomes per proposal.

process_decision: Keep proposal validation as a separate gate layer. Accepted proposal results may feed validated document graph assembly, but review-required and quarantined results must remain visible and must not be promoted without their required action.
process_learning: The validation gate exposed two dominant tuning needs: local boundary confirmation/source collection, and alias conflict reduction. Formula and pivot topology checks are useful here, but formula-result recalculation remains a separate authority and was not performed in this stage.

process_decision: Represent approved authority models as deterministic runtime contracts and health/readiness evidence before adding broader live-smoke automation.

effectiveness_signals: The requester can fill concrete values and attach a validated package before requesting authority changes.

process_decision: Treat authority requests as packaged, evidence-backed changes: decide pilot scope first, then ask SRE/security only for the runtime and governance actions the requester cannot perform alone.
process_learning: Security review gets easier when the requester separates chosen pilot boundaries from approval-only actions and provides both machine-checkable policy and human-readable flow diagrams.
next_adjustment: After SRE/security approval, run the authorized smoke against the pilot spreadsheet and store the resulting health, inspect, and audit evidence in this package.

hypothesis: A connected Google Sheet can be extracted without a separate credential file by using the existing Sheets Bridge local user OAuth cache when the sheet is not publicly exportable and gcloud lacks Sheets API scopes.
actions: Tried public XLSX export and confirmed it returned HTTP 401, so anonymous export was not available.
Tried the current gcloud access token against Sheets API metadata and confirmed it lacked sufficient Sheets API scopes.
Checked the Sheets Bridge local OAuth cache and found configured/authenticated user OAuth with Sheets scope, then refreshed and used it for read-only extraction.
Extracted metadata, values, formulas, and grid snapshots for spreadsheet `1vL4lwW7m6JMewR20WB0-QGoaSNDXIvjHCtyOw8ZeAFQ`, gid `1705521654`, target tab `[4/7] 상세페이지 자료`, range `A1:R31`.
Generated CSV, Markdown preview, extract summary, and extract index artifacts.
Registered the sheet surface in the project registry under `projects/google-sheets/1vL4lwW7m6JMewR20WB0-QGoaSNDXIvjHCtyOw8ZeAFQ/gid-1705521654`.
effectiveness_signals: The extraction used user-authorized OAuth rather than a separate credential file.
All snapshot and manifest JSON artifacts parse successfully.
The extracted tab is a text/material collection sheet rather than a formula-heavy data table; formula and values windows matched at the sampled range level.
process_decision: For keyless extraction attempts, try public export and gcloud scope probes first, then use the MCP-managed local OAuth cache when available and authorized.
process_learning: Keyless does not mean unauthenticated. For non-public Sheets, the practical keyless path is user OAuth or hosted Workload Identity authority; gcloud cloud-platform tokens may still be insufficient for Sheets API.
next_adjustment: Build a single command that performs the same keyless extraction sequence and writes a consolidated package without ad hoc orchestration.
